paper.title: HD144941: The most extreme helium-strong star

review.summary: HD144941 is identified as the most extreme magnetic helium-strong star (not an EHe star). Paper reports FORS2 spectropolarimetry detecting ~9kG longitudinal field, Zeeman splitting confirming >=15kG, hybrid non-LTE model atmosphere analysis yielding Teff=22000K, logg=4.20, He fraction 0.95, Gaia EDR3 parallax confirming massive star nature (~8 Msun), and kinematic analysis identifying it as a runaway star. Contains 4 internal tables (FORS2 observing log, parameter summary, line-by-line NLTE abundances in two parts) and references multiple external data assets (IUE, 2MASS, WISE, Gaia EDR3, K2, ESO spectroscopy, Kurucz atomic data).

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Reports FORS2 spectropolarimetric observing log and derived surface-averaged longitudinal magnetic field measurements for each of 10 observing epochs

source_refs.caption: FORS2 Observing log and average longitudinal magnetic field ($\langle B_z\rangle$) values.
source_refs.label: tab:FOR2log_results

columns.name: Date | YYYY-MM-DD
columns.meaning: UT date of observation

columns.name: JD- | 2450000
columns.meaning: Julian Date minus 2450000 at beginning of exposure sequence

columns.name: No. of | frames
columns.meaning: Number of spectral frames obtained per night

columns.name: Exposure | time (s)
columns.meaning: Exposure time per frame

columns.name: S/N at | 4950 \AA
columns.meaning: Signal-to-noise ratio per pixel of Stokes I spectrum at ~4950A over 100A range

columns.name: \bz (G) | Hydrogen
columns.meaning: Longitudinal magnetic field from hydrogen lines (Stokes V)

columns.name: \nz (G) | All
columns.meaning: Null profile N parameter from hydrogen lines

columns.name: \bz (G)
columns.meaning: Longitudinal magnetic field from full spectrum (Stokes V)

columns.name: \nz (G)
columns.meaning: Null profile N parameter from full spectrum

internal_tables.evidence: FORS2 Observing log and average longitudinal magnetic field values for 10 epochs spanning 2021-03-15 to 2021-07-18.
internal_tables.comments: Table footnotes explain JD reference, number of frames, S/N calculation, and differences between hydrogen-line and full-spectrum Bz/Nz measurements.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Central summary table collecting all derived atmospheric parameters, non-LTE elemental abundances, photometric data, fundamental stellar parameters, and kinematic data for HD144941

source_refs.caption: Parameters and elemental abundances of HD144941.
source_refs.label: tab:parameters

columns.name: Atmospheric parameters:
columns.meaning: Section header for atmospheric parameter block: Teff, logg, helium fraction y, microturbulence xi, v sin i, macroturbulence zeta

columns.name: col_002
columns.meaning: Parameter values and 1-sigma uncertainties for atmospheric parameters (Teff=22000±500K, logg=4.20±0.10, y=0.950±0.002, xi=2±1km/s, v sin i=7±5km/s, zeta=25/15±5km/s)

columns.name: col_003
columns.meaning: Non-LTE abundances log n_X normalized to sum n_X=1 for H, C, N, O, Ne, Mg, Al, Si, S, Fe; photometric data V, B-V, E(B-V), R_V, M_V, M_bol; fundamental parameters M, R, logL, tau, tau/tau_MS, P_rot; kinematic data parallax, proper motions, radial velocity, distances

columns.name: col_004
columns.meaning: Additional parameter values and references for photometric/kinematic data (e.g., microturbulence footnote a, photometry reference b, rotation period reference c, Gaia reference d)

internal_tables.evidence: Multi-block summary table: Atmospheric parameters section with Teff=22000±500K, logg=4.20±0.10, y=0.950±0.002; Non-LTE abundances for 10 elements; Photometric data from Slawsonetal92; Fundamental parameters M=8.1±0.3Msun, R=3.8±0.2Rsun; Kinematic data from Gaia EDR3.
internal_tables.comments: This is the paper's primary summary table. The 4-column layout groups parameters into blocks: atmospheric, abundance, photometric, fundamental, kinematic. Table footnotes reference external data sources (Slawsonetal92, JeRa18, Gaia).

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents detailed line-by-line non-LTE abundance measurements for H, C, N, O, and Ne (first part of the appendix abundance table)

source_refs.caption: Line-by-line abundances from the non-LTE analysis of HD144941. Abundances $\log n_X$ (normalised to $\sum n_X$\,=\,1). \label{tab:abundances_lines_nlte}
source_refs.label: tab:abundances_lines_nlte

columns.name: Line
columns.meaning: Spectral line identification (ion and multiplet)

columns.name: \lambda (\AA)
columns.meaning: Rest-frame wavelength of the spectral line

columns.name: \chi(eV)
columns.meaning: Excitation energy of the lower level of the transition

columns.name: \log gf
columns.meaning: Logarithm of the oscillator strength times statistical weight

columns.name: Accu.
columns.meaning: Accuracy flag for the gf-value (AAA=0.3%, AA=1%, A=3%, B=10%, C=25%, D=50%, E>50%, X=unknown)

columns.name: Source
columns.meaning: Bibliographic source code for the gf-value (WF, WFD, FFT, MAR, K14, etc. — see table footnote for full references)

columns.name: \log n_X
columns.meaning: Non-LTE abundance log n_X normalized to sum of all number fractions = 1

internal_tables.evidence: Line-by-line NLTE abundances for H (Balmer lines Hdelta-Halpha), C II (9 transitions), N II (17 transitions), O I/II (25 transitions), Ne I (2 transitions). Includes gf-value accuracy grades and source references.
internal_tables.comments: First of two consecutive tables in the appendix. Table footnote lists the gf-value accuracy scale and bibliographic source codes.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Continues the line-by-line non-LTE abundance measurements for Mg, Al, Si, S, and Fe (second part of the appendix abundance table)

columns.name: Line
columns.meaning: Spectral line identification (ion and multiplet)

columns.name: \lambda (\AA)
columns.meaning: Rest-frame wavelength of the spectral line

columns.name: \chi(eV)
columns.meaning: Excitation energy of the lower level of the transition

columns.name: \log gf
columns.meaning: Logarithm of the oscillator strength times statistical weight

columns.name: Accu.
columns.meaning: Accuracy flag for the gf-value (AAA=0.3%, AA=1%, A=3%, B=10%, C=25%, D=50%, E>50%, X=unknown)

columns.name: Source
columns.meaning: Bibliographic source code for the gf-value (FW, FFTI, KP, WSM, KB, etc. — see table footnote for full references)

internal_tables.evidence: Continuation: line-by-line NLTE abundances for Mg II (1 multiplet), Al III (4 transitions), Si II/III (21 transitions), S II/III (5 transitions), Fe III (multiple transitions).
internal_tables.comments: Continuation of tab:abundances_lines_nlte. Contains the gf-value source footnote with bibliographic references including the Kurucz URL (http://kurucz.harvard.edu/atoms.html) and other atomic data references.

external_resources.description: IUE low-dispersion large-aperture UV spectrophotometry datasets SWP07696 and LWR06703, used to constrain the spectral energy distribution of HD144941

source_refs.context: Low-dispersion and large-aperture spectrophotometry from the International Ultraviolet Exporer (IUE) was employed to constrain the spectral energy distribution (SED) of HD144941 (data sets SWP07696 and LWR06703).

external_resources.evidence: Text in Observations section: IUE data sets SWP07696 and LWR06703 used for SED fitting.
external_resources.comments: IUE final archive at MAST: https://archive.stsci.edu/iue/

external_resources.description: Two Micron All Sky Survey (2MASS) JHK-band photometry for HD144941, supplementing the SED constraints

source_refs.context: in the Two Micron All Sky Survey (2MASS) $JHK$-bands from \citet{2MASS2006}

external_resources.evidence: Text in Observations section: 2MASS JHK photometry from Skrutskie et al. 2006.

external_resources.description: Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE) ALLWISE photometry for HD144941, supplementing the SED in thermal infrared

source_refs.context: Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE) bands from \citet{ALLWISE_vizier}

external_resources.evidence: Text in Observations section: WISE ALLWISE photometry from Cutri et al. 2021 (VizieR II/328).
external_resources.comments: ALLWISE data release, available via VizieR catalog II/328 and IRSA

external_resources.description: Johnson UBV-band photometry for HD144941 from Slawson, Hill & Landstreet (1992, ApJS, 82, 117): V=10.141±0.008, B-V=0.034±0.007
external_resources.evidence: Text in Observations section + Table tab:parameters footnotes b: V and B-V values from Slawsonetal92.
external_resources.comments: Ground-based UBV photometry used for SED fitting and absolute magnitude calibration

external_resources.description: Gaia Early Data Release 3 astrometry for HD144941: parallax pi=0.6714±0.0282 mas, proper motions mu_alpha=-6.834±0.034 mas/yr, mu_delta=8.023±0.025 mas/yr

source_refs.context: Parallax $\pi_\mathrm{EDR3}$ and proper motion data $\mu_\alpha$ and $\mu_\delta$ from Gaia Early Data Release 3

external_resources.evidence: Table tab:parameters footnotes d and kinematic analysis section: Gaia EDR3 data from Gaia Collaboration (2016, 2021), parallax zero-point correction from Ren et al. 2021.
external_resources.comments: Gaia EDR3 source ID not explicitly stated. Used to derive distance d_Gaia=1556pc and kinematic orbit.

external_resources.description: K2 mission light curve of HD144941 showing rotational modulation with a fundamental period of 13.93d, previously published by Jeffery & Ramsay (2018)

source_refs.context: \citet{JeRa18} found a highly structured singly periodic signal in K2 observations, with a fundamental period of 13.9$\pm$0.2\,d

external_resources.evidence: Magnetic field section and Table tab:parameters footnote c: rotation period from K2 light curve (JeRa18).
external_resources.comments: K2 data used to phase magnetic field measurements. Rotation period adopted as 13.93d.

external_resources.description: ESO VLT FORS2 spectropolarimetric observations of HD144941 under programmes 105.205U.001 and 106.211U.001 (March–July 2021), 10 epochs with grism 600B, R~1700, 3250-6215A

source_refs.context: Our target star HD144941 was observed in service mode on a total of nine nights in March, June, and July 2021 using the FOcal Reducer and low dispersion Spectrograph 2 (FORS2)
Based on observations collected at the European Southern Observatory under ESO programmes 077.D-0458(A,B), 105.205U.001 and 106.211U.001.

external_resources.evidence: Observations section and acknowledgements: ESO programmes 105.205U.001, 106.211U.001 for FORS2 spectropolarimetry.
external_resources.comments: ESO archive data. FORS2 data reduced with custom pipeline based on Fossati et al. 2015 and Bagnulo et al. 2012.

external_resources.description: ESO FEROS high-resolution optical spectrum (R=48000) of HD144941 from 2006-04-08, 3000s exposure, S/N~200, under ESO programme 077.D-0458(A,B)

source_refs.context: An optical spectrum with $R$\,=\,48\,000 was obtained on April 8, 2006 using the Fibre-fed Optical Echelle Spectrograph [FEROS]

external_resources.evidence: Observations section: FEROS spectrum on ESO-MPG 2.2m telescope at La Silla.
external_resources.comments: ESO programme 077.D-0458(A,B). Normalised using sdB star HD188112 spectrum.

external_resources.description: ESO VLT UVES high-resolution optical-UV spectrum (R~41000) of HD144941 from 2006-04-10, 780s exposure, S/N~170, covering ~3200-8000A

source_refs.context: a 780\,s exposure with the Ultraviolet and Visual Echelle Spectrograph [UVES] on the ESO-VLT UT2 on April 10, 2006

external_resources.evidence: Observations section: UVES spectrum. Normalised using DC white dwarf WD1917-07.
external_resources.comments: ESO programme 077.D-0458(A,B). Combined FEROS+UVES wavelength coverage ~3200-8000A.

external_resources.description: Kurucz atomic data (energy levels, gf-values) for Si III lines, referenced as source codes K13 and K14 in the line abundance table footnote
external_resources.evidence: Table footnote in the continued abundance table: source code K13, K14 for gf-values refer to Kurucz website.
external_resources.comments: Canonical Kurucz atomic data repository. Used for Si III oscillator strengths.
